name: tensorkube
documentation: # Serverless GPUS on your own cloud

functions.name: tensorkube.services.dataset_service.upload_tensorkube_dataset
documentation:
Uploads a dataset file to S3 with progress bar

Args:
    dataset_id: Unique identifier for the dataset
    file_path: Path to the local JSONL file

Returns:
    bool: True if upload was successful, False otherwise

functions.name: tensorkube.services.dataset_service.delete_tensorkube_dataset
documentation:
Deletes a dataset from S3 using its ID

Args:
    dataset_id: ID of the dataset to delete

Returns:
    bool: True if deletion was successful, False otherwise

functions.name: tensorkube.services.job_queue_service.get_queued_message
documentation: EXPOSE

functions.name: tensorkube.services.job_queue_service.set_job_status
documentation: EXPOSE

functions.name: tensorkube.services.job_queue_service.get_job_status
documentation: EXPOSE

functions.name: tensorkube.services.cloudformation_service.cloudformation
documentation: Create a cloudformation stack.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.kubectl_utils.check_and_install_kubectl
documentation: Check if kubectl is installed and install it if it's not.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.kubectl_utils.check_and_install_helm
documentation: Check if helm is installed and install it if it's not.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.istio.check_and_install_istioctl
documentation: Check if istioctl is installed and install it if it's not.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.istio.install_istio_on_cluster
documentation: Install Istio with the default profile.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.istio.uninstall_istio_from_cluster
documentation: Uninstall Istio from the cluster.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.rich_service.display_job_logs
documentation:
Displays logs from a specified job's pods

Args:
    job_prefix: Prefix of the job name
    namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the job exists
    container_name: Name of the container whose logs to display

functions.name: tensorkube.services.rich_service.list_tensorkube_training_jobs
documentation:
Lists all jobs and their statuses with optional prefix filtering

Args:
    namespace: Specific namespace to list jobs from
    all: If True, lists jobs from all namespaces
    job_prefix: Optional prefix to filter job names

functions.name: tensorkube.services.rich_service.get_tensorkube_training_job
documentation:
Get job details

Args:
    job_prefix: Prefix of the job name
    namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the job exists

functions.name: tensorkube.services.rich_service.delete_tensorkube_job_by_prefix
documentation:
Deletes a job matching the given prefix. If multiple jobs match, asks user to select one.

Args:
    job_prefix: Prefix of the job name to delete
    namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the job exists

Returns:
    bool: True if deletion was successful, False otherwise

functions.name: tensorkube.services.eksctl_service.create_base_tensorkube_cluster_eksctl
documentation: create base cluster using eksctl

functions.name: tensorkube.services.eksctl_service.check_and_install_eksctl
documentation: Check if eksctl is installed and if not isntall it.

functions.name: tensorkube.services.k8s_service.list_jobs
documentation:
Lists Kubernetes jobs in a namespace or across all namespaces with optional job name prefix filtering

Args:
    namespace: Specific namespace to list jobs from
    all: If True, lists jobs from all namespaces
    context_name: Kubernetes context name to use
    job_name_prefix: Optional prefix to filter job names

Returns:
    Dict containing list of jobs or None if context not found

functions.name: tensorkube.services.k8s_service.delete_job
documentation:
Stops and deletes a Kubernetes job and its associated pods

Args:
    job_name: Name of the job to delete
    namespace: Kubernetes namespace where the job exists

Returns:
    bool: True if deletion was successful, False otherwise

functions.name: tensorkube.services.train.convert_dataset_to_s3_path
documentation: todo: currently supporting one dataset inside datasets

variables.name: tensorkube.services.train.supported_base_models
documentation: from hugging face

functions.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.create_s3_bucket
documentation: NOTE: bucket name must be universally unique
NOTE: bucket name should not be guessable

functions.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.list_bucket_contents
documentation:
Lists all objects in an S3 bucket with detailed, human-readable information.

Args:
    bucket_name: Name of the S3 bucket
    prefix: Optional prefix to filter objects (like a directory path)
    region: AWS region where the bucket is located

Returns:
    List of S3ObjectInfo containing human-readable file/directory information

functions.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.delete_file_from_s3
documentation:
Deletes a single file from an S3 bucket

Args:
    bucket_name: Name of the S3 bucket
    object_name: Name of the file to delete
    region: AWS region where the bucket is located

Returns:
    bool: True if deletion was successful, False otherwise

methods.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.S3ObjectInfo.from_response
documentation: Creates an S3ObjectInfo instance from S3 response objects with human-readable formats

class_variables.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.S3ObjectInfo.last_modified
documentation: Human readable time

class_variables.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.S3ObjectInfo.size
documentation: Human readable size

class_variables.name: tensorkube.services.s3_service.S3ObjectInfo.raw_size
documentation: Original size in bytes

functions.name: tensorkube.services.cli.teardown
documentation: The following commands can tear down all the resources that you have created and configured using the CLI.
uninstall knative
uninstall istio
uninstall karpenter
delete cluster

functions.name: tensorkube.services.cli.deploy
documentation:
Deploy your containerized application on Tensorkube. This command requires
a dockerfile to be present in the current directory.

documentation: Describe a specific deployment...

functions.name: tensorkube.services.knative_service.enable_knative_selectors_pv_pvc_capabilities
documentation:
Enable the nodeSelector feature in the config-features ConfigMap.

Args:
    namespace (str): The namespace where the ConfigMap is located. Defaults to 'knative-serving'.
